body:
When using the `GPU`, launching a grid-sized kernel with an immersed boundary implies computing the kernel for all the i, j, and k indices since we use non-branching if conditions.

This is quite detrimental in case of large-compute bound kernels such as tendency calculations.

This PR removes the tendency calculations in the immersed regions by passing an `active_cells_map` that allows to iterate only on wet cells

labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster
labels.name: performance 🏍️

labels.description: Where Oceananigans gets its powers from
labels.name: GPU 👾

title: Improve immersed boundary performance
